name: Geodetic and frame-dragging precession (Gravity Probe B)
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the geodetic (de Sitter) and Lense-Thirring (frame-dragging) gyroscope precessions in Earth orbit, as measured by Gravity Probe B and confirmed by satellite laser ranging (LARES, LAGEOS).

regime.description: Polar Earth orbit at 642 km altitude; weak-field, slow-rotation regime.

current_bound.value: geodetic: -6601.8 +/- 18.3 mas/yr (GR predicts -6606.1); frame-dragging: -37.2 +/- 7.2 mas/yr (GR predicts -39.2)
current_bound.experiment: Gravity Probe B (4 cryogenic gyroscopes in Earth orbit)
current_bound.reference: Everitt et al., 'Gravity Probe B: Final Results of a Space Experiment to Test General Relativity', PRL 106, 221101 (2011), arXiv:1105.3456

what_it_excludes: Frameworks predicting deviations in either precession at the few-percent level are excluded. LARES + LAGEOS independently confirm the frame-dragging effect to ~5%, providing a cross-check of any modification of GR's gravitomagnetic sector.

parameterization.name: PPN parameters gamma (geodetic) and (1 + gamma + alpha_1/4) (frame-dragging combination).
parameterization.summary: Geodetic precession scales with gamma; frame-dragging tests a specific PPN combination including alpha_1.

procedural.predicted_observable: Geodetic: Omega_geo = (1 + 2 gamma)/3 * (3 G M / 2 c^2 r^3) v cross r. Frame-dragging: Omega_LT = (1 + gamma + alpha_1/4)/2 * (G I omega / c^2 r^3).
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Same gamma constraint as Cassini (gamma - 1 = (2.1 +/- 2.3) x 10^-5). Frame-dragging combination tested to ~20% by GP-B alone.
framework_inputs: PPN parameters as in recover_general_relativity entry; frame-dragging requires the off-diagonal g_0i metric component at 1.5PN.

primary_references.citation: Everitt et al., 'Gravity Probe B: Final Results of a Space Experiment to Test General Relativity', PRL 106, 221101 (2011)
Ciufolini et al., 'A test of general relativity using the LARES and LAGEOS satellites and a GRACE Earth gravity model', EPJC 76, 120 (2016)
